bear_case: AAPL already touched $292.13 yesterday — intraday volatility is sufficient to clear $290 if early buying emerges. Any positive macro (jobs data) could lift tech broadly.

market: Will Apple (AAPL) finish week of May 4 above $290?
thesis: AAPL at $287.44 needs +0.89% to clear $290. Closed near session lows ($285.78-$292.13 range) suggesting selling pressure. Market at 46¢ YES overprices the probability by ~9pts. Pre-market slightly negative (-0.02%). No obvious catalyst for a gap-up.

resolution_trap: Potential overnight price update or late session rally not fully reflected in latest $90.50 data

thesis: WTI at ~$90.50 needs a +3.8% single-session rally to close above $94. This magnitude of daily move occurs <5% of sessions historically. Market at 72¢ YES is wildly mispriced — likely stale or reflecting pre-decline positioning. NO at 28¢ offers massive edge (~60pts).

bear_case: Lithuania has historically been inconsistent at Eurovision; they could easily miss qualification. No data on competitive field strength in this semi-final.
key_factors: No song quality or rehearsal data available
Market price aligns with base rate — no edge identified
market: Will Lithuania advance through the first Eurovision Semi-Final?
thesis: Base rate for semi-final qualification is ~62-67% (10 of 15-16 countries advance). Market at 64¢ is priced very close to base rate. Without rehearsal reviews, betting odds, or song reception data, no meaningful edge exists either way.

bear_case: Don't even know what YES resolves to. Tiny $98 volume suggests illiquid market with unreliable pricing. Match outcome between two Associate nations is inherently unpredictable.

thesis: Match starts in ~77 minutes. Both are minor Associate cricket nations. No team form data, player stats, or even clear resolution criteria (what YES means) available. Market at 76¢ may reflect informed bettors but cannot verify. Resolution risk is high due to unclear proposition.

market: Will Apple (AAPL) finish week of May 4 above $290?
thesis: AAPL at $287.44 needs +0.87% on Friday to close above $290. It touched $292.13 intraday but closed near the low at $287.51, signaling bearish end-of-day momentum. Market at 46¢ YES overprices the probability given the stock needs a specific upward move from below the strike. NO at 54¢ vs my ~62% fair value gives ~8pt edge.

bear_case: Indiana Fever roster improvements and Caitlin Clark's sophomore leap could make them dominant. Season openers often favor hyped teams with higher motivation.
key_factors: Preseason Dallas win 95-80 is minor signal
Market consensus likely informed by betting lines we don't have
thesis: Market at 29¢ is roughly fair. Preseason win (95-80) provides a mild data point for Dallas, but preseason results are weak predictors. Indiana with Caitlin Clark in Year 2 is likely favored for good reason. No meaningful edge identified.

bear_case: Lithuania has historically qualified more often than not, and the song is in Spanish which could be distinctive. 64% may be accurate or even slightly low.

market: Will Lithuania advance through the first Eurovision Semi-Final?
thesis: Base rate for advancing is ~59-67% (10 of ~16 acts qualify). Lithuania is a mid-tier competitor. Odds declining from 70% to 64% since January could signal weakening competitive position. My estimate of 61% is slightly below market's 64%, but insufficient edge to justify a trade given low evidence quality.

thesis: No edge detected. Historical base rate (~64%) matches market price almost exactly. Without current betting odds, expert assessments, or competition field analysis, cannot establish meaningful deviation from market consensus.
